description: The CAT OS content registry. Every content item ever created has exactly one entry here; no item exists outside it, and no entry exists without a file. Fields follow CONTENT_GENERATION_PIPELINE.md section 2, extended with the fields the schemas require (estimated_time_min; stage since v2; difficulty_numeric since 0.6.0 so the journey can order passages truthfully within a stage). Updated once per batch and committed with the batch's content files.

items.title: The Discipline of Deferring
items.theme: epistemic deference and intellectual autonomy
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Milestone 2)
items.reviewer_notes: Authored during Milestone 2; owner review pending.

items.theme: prices as decentralized knowledge transmission
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Milestone 2)
items.reviewer_notes: Authored during Milestone 2; owner review pending.

items.theme: replication and the epistemic status of a single result
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Milestone 2)
items.reviewer_notes: Authored during Milestone 2; hard 5-question item; owner review pending.

items.title: The Measured and the Managed
items.theme: measurement distorts the behaviour it measures
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Milestone 2)
items.reviewer_notes: Authored during Milestone 2; owner review pending.

items.title: Reading It Again
items.theme: rereading and the difference between plot and form
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Milestone 2)
items.reviewer_notes: Authored during Milestone 2 as the batch's easy item; owner review pending.

items.title: What the Hand Remembers
items.theme: procedural versus declarative memory, and why understanding is not ability
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Reading Experience milestone)
items.reviewer_notes: The journey's opening passage: deliberately gentle and concrete. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Invention of the Weekend
items.theme: the weekend as a negotiated invention rather than a natural rhythm
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Reading Experience milestone)
items.reviewer_notes: First history item; second intermediate passage. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Authority of the Original
items.theme: translation as interpretation made answerable, and the relocated authority of the original
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Reading Experience milestone)
items.reviewer_notes: The library's first elite passage; hardest register, 5 questions. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Ethics of Looking
items.theme: attention as the site of moral work that precedes choice
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Reported-view opening with a late, deliberately qualified thesis; the main-idea near-tie is between the correct modest claim and its inflated twin. Owner review pending.

items.title: Words That Do No Work
items.theme: small talk as connective ritual rather than information exchange
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage passage: one concept, concrete evidence, a warm register; the traps train the information-vs-function distinction. Owner review pending.

items.title: Why the Quartet Costs More
items.theme: why labour-intensive services grow costlier as an economy grows more productive
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Counter-intuitive economics with a mechanism the reader must hold across paragraphs; the main-idea trap inflates a relative claim into a claim about absolute decline. Owner review pending.

items.title: More Than the Sum
items.theme: emergence and the limits of reductive explanation
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Elite-register science-ideas passage with an understated thesis that occupies a middle position; the central near-tie is between the author's qualified emergentism and a stronger anti-reductionism the author declines. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Comfort of the Familiar
items.theme: familiarity itself, apart from quality, makes us like things more
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage psychology: one clean finding developed concretely. Distractors train the separation of 'familiarity' from 'quality' and from 'conscious recognition'. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Consent of the Defeated
items.theme: the losing side's willing consent as the hidden mechanism of democracy
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Reframing passage: the visible act (voting) is demoted, the invisible one (losers' consent) promoted. Main-idea trap keeps the reader on the winners. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Outsourced Conscience
items.theme: the erosion of moral skill when judgment is delegated to automated systems
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Hard technology-ethics with a subtle thesis (not that machines decide badly, but that our capacity to decide atrophies). Includes a strengthen/weaken item on the load-bearing assumption. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Strength of Weak Ties
items.theme: why loose acquaintances, not close friends, are the bridges that carry new information
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Counter-intuitive sociology; the reader must grasp that the argument is about the structural POSITION of weak ties, not their emotional warmth. Owner review pending.

items.title: Setting the Nation's Clocks
items.theme: the standardization of clock time as a practical response to the railway, not a natural fact
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage history-of-ideas: a familiar thing (clock time) shown to be a recent invention. Distractors train the difference between the cause given and plausible alternatives. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Trouble with the Pristine
items.theme: wilderness as a cultural construction whose idealisation distorts conservation
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Elite environmental-humanities passage; the thesis criticises an idea beloved by environmentalists, and the central trap is to read it as anti-environmental. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Original and Its Ghost
items.theme: why a perfect copy does not replace an original: value as history, not appearance
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Arts-culture with a clean but non-obvious thesis (value as history, not appearance). Main-idea trap keeps the reader on visual fidelity. Owner review pending.

items.title: Who Owns a Poem's Meaning
items.theme: whether an author's intention fixes a text's meaning, and where interpretation's limits lie
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Literature-theory with a two-sided thesis: intention doesn't govern meaning, but interpretation isn't unlimited. The central trap is the 'anything goes' overshoot. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Market That Ate Itself
items.theme: how asymmetric information can unravel a market entirely
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Hard economics with a self-reinforcing mechanism (adverse selection) the reader must track step by step; includes a strengthen/weaken item on the load-bearing information assumption. Owner review pending.

items.title: Why the Past Keeps Changing
items.theme: why history is continually rewritten without thereby being unreliable
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Historiography passage with a careful middle position: history is revised, but this is not relativism. Main-idea trap is the cynical 'history is just opinion' overshoot. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Fortunes of the Guilty
items.theme: the paradox of moral luck and the limits of the control principle
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Hard philosophy: a paradox the author leaves unresolved. The main trap is to read a resolution the author declines to offer; tone must catch the deliberate discomfort. Owner review pending.

items.title: Believing What We Have Done
items.theme: how we revise beliefs to justify actions we have already taken
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: The counter-intuitive direction (action shapes belief, not only belief action) is the crux; distractors reverse it or over-generalise. Owner review pending.

items.title: Who Are the People?
items.theme: the boundary problem: democracy cannot democratically decide who belongs to the people
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Elite political theory built on a self-referential paradox; the main trap is a proposed 'solution' the author shows only relocates the problem. Owner review pending.

items.title: No Such Thing as a Free Gift
items.theme: the gift as a binding social obligation rather than a free transfer
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Anthropology of exchange; the crux is that gifts BIND rather than being free. Distractors read the gift as pure altruism or as disguised market trade. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Panic and the Threat
items.theme: how a moral panic amplifies a threat through reaction rather than reality
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage sociology: one clear concept with a self-amplifying loop. Distractors confuse the panic's size with the threat's size. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Swan and the Test
items.theme: the logical asymmetry between confirming and refuting a general claim
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Philosophy-of-science passage on the confirm/refute asymmetry; the crux is that a theory earns credit by surviving tests meant to kill it, not by accumulating confirmations. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Freedom of the Fourteen Lines
items.theme: why artistic constraints enable rather than restrict creativity
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage arts passage; the counter-intuitive claim (limits help) is stated plainly, with a careful qualifier the traps ignore. Owner review pending.

items.title: When You Are the Product
items.theme: when attention is the product, engagement diverges from the user's interest
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Attention-economy passage; the crux is the divergence between engagement and the user's interest. Distractors moralize the companies or over-claim manipulation. Owner review pending.

items.title: Crying at the Cinema
items.theme: why we feel genuine emotions for characters we know to be fictional
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Foundation-stage aesthetics; one clean paradox and a proposed resolution. Distractors confuse 'we know it's fiction' with 'we don't really feel'. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Price of a Forest
items.theme: the promise and the hidden cost of assigning nature a monetary value
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Premium Reading Library milestone); RC_MASTER_GENERATION_PROMPT.v1 + CAT_VARC_BIBLE craft rules applied
items.reviewer_notes: Environment passage weighing a strategy's benefit against its subtle cost; the author reaches a genuinely balanced verdict. Main trap is to read it as simply for or against pricing nature. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Machine That Taught Europe to Read
items.theme: how the printing press made reading ordinary
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: The journey's opening jumble: a clean chronology with one pronoun and one explicit closer. Deliberately gentle; teaches the spine-first habit. Owner review pending.

items.title: What the Sleeping Brain Is Busy Doing
items.theme: sleep as an active part of learning
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Beginner claim-evidence shape with an explicit demonstrative closer. Teaches that reasons come before proofs. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Quiet Rules of Waiting in Line
items.theme: queues as unspoken social contracts
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Beginner general-to-specific with a first, gentle taste of the repeated-word decoy. The demonstrative resolves it cleanly. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Loop That Runs You
items.theme: how habits take over from conscious choice
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Easy-tier item teaching the article rule honestly: a introduces, the continues. One gentle competing opener. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Oldest Air Conditioner
items.theme: urban trees as the cheapest answer to city heat
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Easy-tier problem-solution with a synonym bridge (trees to canopy) as the key join. Gentle heat-cool decoy included. Owner review pending.

items.title: Ends Sunday
items.theme: how deadlines manufacture desire in retail
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Easy-tier closer: a reference chain (the discovery, the same lever, none of this) that must be walked in order. First taste of the false pair. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Fight in Slogans
items.theme: what research actually says about the minimum wage
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: First engineered competing opener: the vivid slogans sentence is built to look like a first line. A pure surface solver opens with it and loses. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Word That Went Missing
items.theme: how a single mistranslation built a myth about spinach
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Engineered lexical decoy: iron and spinach repeat across a non-adjacent pair; the true neighbour links by cause. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Face That Was Never Told
items.theme: the misinformation effect and the fragility of memory
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Medium item with a false pronoun binding: it could attach to memory or to the experiment; the author's subject decides. Long reference chain. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Ship That Was Rebuilt
items.theme: identity and the paradox of gradual replacement
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Advanced item: implicit relations, an abstract topic, and a however that could attach to more than one sentence. Ordering must come from the argument's shape. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Colour That Cost a Fortune
items.theme: how the scarcity of a pigment shaped what art could say
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Advanced item engineering the generic-the trap: the true opener begins with The, defeating the mechanical article heuristic. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Tyranny of Small Decisions
items.theme: how individually rational choices produce a collectively irrational result
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: CAT-tier item: abstract topic, implicit relations, two plausible openers and two plausible endings, several locally coherent orders. Only the full arc resolves it. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Reader Finishes the Book
items.theme: why a text's meaning is completed by the reader, not fixed by the writer
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: CAT-tier item with an engineered anchor pair (B-D share writer/reader vocabulary and read smoothly adjacent) that the true order splits. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Clock in the Cell
items.theme: why the body's internal clock resists sudden change
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: CAT+ item stacking a lexical decoy (light/clock repeats) with a false pronoun binding (they). Two ending candidates. Only the mechanism resolves it. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Debt That Came Before Money
items.theme: why obligation, not barter, was the origin of economic life
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: CAT+ item where the vivid reported view (A, the textbook barter story) is written to look like the thesis, and a however (C) could attach to two sentences. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Map and the Territory
items.theme: why a model's usefulness depends on what it leaves out
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: 99-percentile item: abstract, implicit relations, multiple locally coherent orders. Only reading each candidate whole to check the given-new spine resolves it. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Bias in the Mirror
items.theme: why a neutral algorithm can still inherit an unjust past
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: 99-percentile item with a five-link dependency chain and a pronoun (it) that the system and the past both plausibly claim. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Silence in the Archive
items.theme: why the gaps in a historical record are themselves evidence
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Premium item: a generic-the opener, an anchor pair of tonally matched abstract sentences, and two plausible closers, all at once. Every heuristic is tested; only the full argument resolves. Owner review pending.

items.title: The Cost You Cannot See
items.theme: why the true price of a choice is the option it forecloses
items.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Para Jumbles milestone); PARA_JUMBLES_BIBLE frameworks applied
items.reviewer_notes: Premium item: the word cost repeats across nearly every sentence as a dense decoy, while the true joins run on logic and a former/latter reference. Owner review pending.
